title: Review book chapters 10-15 (Part III) for protocol consistency and scaffold usage
markdown:
## Goal

Review chapters 10 through 15 of "What's in a Message" (Part III: The Network) for consistency with the current B3nd/Firecat protocol state. This is the most technically dense part of the book and the most likely to have drifted from the codebase. Ensure the three-layer scaffold (speech -> paper -> digital/B3nd) is used consistently in every chapter.

## Background

Part III is where the book transitions from paper-based metaphors to actual B3nd protocol concepts. Chapters cover: the node (Ch 10), real-time communication (Ch 11), agreements/transactions (Ch 12), consensus (Ch 13), extensibility (Ch 14), and the "everything is a message" unifying idea (Ch 15).

Several protocol changes have occurred since the book was written:
- The confirmation protocol was designed and validators partially implemented (`libs/firecat-protocol/CONFIRMATION.md`)
- Temporal consensus was designed with era/block/slot coordinates
- Gas semantics and UTXO model were proposed (tokenization-gas-semantics.md)
- The economic model was drafted with three revenue streams
- `respondTo()` and `connect()` compose primitives were designed but not yet built

The book should accurately reflect what exists and what is designed, without overstating implementation status. Any description of consensus, economics, or multi-node behavior must align with the current design documents.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each chapter 10-15 uses the three-layer scaffold consistently (speech -> paper -> B3nd for each major concept)
- [ ] Protocol terminology matches current usage in `skills/b3nd/FIRECAT.md` and `skills/b3nd/FRAMEWORK.md`
- [ ] URI scheme names, operation names (`receive`, `read`, `list`, `delete`, `send`), and program names match current SDK
- [ ] The consensus chapter (Ch 13) is consistent with `libs/firecat-protocol/CONFIRMATION.md` and `docs/book/consensus/` materials
- [ ] No chapter claims features are implemented that are still proposals/designs
- [ ] The "everything is a message" chapter (Ch 15) correctly describes the current message-as-universal-primitive philosophy
- [ ] Any code snippets in Part III use current SDK API (import paths, function signatures)
- [ ] Corrections are applied directly to the chapter files

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `skills/b3nd/FIRECAT.md` (full file) to refresh current canonical schema, programs, and URI patterns
2. Read `skills/b3nd/FRAMEWORK.md` to refresh current SDK primitives, types, and composition patterns
3. Read `libs/firecat-protocol/CONFIRMATION.md` for current confirmation protocol design
4. Read `docs/book/consensus/README.md` and related consensus docs for context on what the book says about consensus
5. Read chapters 10-15 sequentially. For each chapter:
   - Verify the three-layer scaffold is present and consistent
   - Check all protocol terminology against current source of truth
   - Check any code snippets against actual SDK API (import paths like `@bandeira-tech/b3nd-sdk`, function names, type names)
   - Verify claims about what the protocol does/provides match reality
   - Note any sections that reference features not yet implemented -- these should use future tense or be clearly marked as design goals
6. Cross-reference chapter 13 (consensus) with `libs/firecat-protocol/CONFIRMATION.md` for accuracy
7. Cross-reference chapter 14 (bending the machine) with the compose layer (`libs/b3nd-compose/`) for accuracy
8. Fix all issues directly in the chapter files
9. Document significant changes in the PR description

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/book/10-the-machine-that-reads-mail.md` through `docs/book/15-everything-is-a-message.md` -- fix protocol inconsistencies, stale terminology, incorrect code snippets, scaffold gaps
- `docs/book/consensus/README.md` -- update if it has drifted from the confirmation protocol design

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Nothing -- this review can proceed independently
- **Blocks**: G4-F1-03 (publish plan depends on the book being review-complete)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Review all 16 chapters for completeness and consistency with current protocol state
- [ ] Ensure three-layer scaffold is used consistently in every chapter from Part II onward
- [ ] Review cookbook chapter (Ch 16) against actual current SDK API
- [ ] Publish/distribute (web reader in rig? PDF? separate site?)
